2606.29158.title: On the Nonlinearity of Learning Rate Scaling for LLM Training
2606.29158.paper_summary: Learning-rate transfer can reduce the cost of training large language models: instead of sweeping learning rates at target scale, practitioners extrapolate from smaller runs. Existing approaches often assume that the optimal learning rate follows a log-linear scaling law in data scale and model size. We carefully examine and evaluate this scaling law. In our empirical study of GPT-2--style models from 22M to 707M parameters trained on 5B to 100B tokens, the optimal learning rate develops upward curvature at larger scales, leading to inaccurate extrapolation. We find that this curvature largely disappears when learning rates are replaced by effective learning rate (the step size in normalized weight space), and when data $D$ extrapolation is used instead of model size $N$ extrapolation. Next, we explain nonlinearity in scaling: weight-norm converges to equilibrium slower when optimal learning is small, requiring a larger step size to reduce the transient phase. Experiments with AdamH, which directly controls the effective learning rate, further support this explanation.
2606.29158.paper_summary_zh: 學習率轉移可以降低訓練大型語言模型的成本：實踐者不再在目標規模上進行學習率的全面搜尋，而是從較小的運行中進行外推。現有的方法通常假設最佳學習率遵循數據規模和模型大小的對數線性縮放定律。我們仔細檢查並評估這一縮放定律。在我們對22M到707M參數的GPT-2風格模型進行的實證研究中，這些模型在5B到100B的標記上進行訓練，最佳學習率在較大規模下出現上凹的曲率，導致不準確的外推。我們發現，當學習率被有效學習率（在標準化權重空間中的步長）取代時，這種曲率在很大程度上消失，並且當使用數據$D$外推而不是模型大小$N$外推時。接下來，我們解釋了縮放中的非線性：當最佳學習率較小時，權重範數收斂到平衡的速度較慢，需要更大的步長來減少過渡階段。對直接控制有效學習率的AdamH的實驗進一步支持了這一解釋。
2606.29158.author: Zaiwen Yang et.al.
2606.29158.authors: Zaiwen Yang, Huaqing Zhang, Jing Xu, Jingzhao Zhang
